code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test05-MAY-2016Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, indicating possible impaired renal function. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, glucosuria, mild ketonuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine color/appearance, suggesting renal tubular dysfunction or possible nephrotic syndrome. HbA1c is low, making chronic hyperglycemia unlikely. No evidence of urinary tract infection. Recommend further nephrological evaluation and assessment for underlying renal pathology.
